item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one, but the ending where the hand is tipped kinda sours it for me. I feel like it would be stronger if it remains implying the meme is driving people to fatten up for something, but leave it up to the reader to decide what. Still got my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same shot of chickens as before is displayed. Children have disappeared. One chicken has scraps of cloth stuck in its claws. SCP-2078-1 then re-enters screen.

These implications, though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm upset with two things, starting with the expungements and blanks. I mean, most of them are just withdrawing info that would actually help to improve the skip. As a rule of thumb, a censor in the article should either serve a purpose in the story or just withdraw irrelevant details. But here, most of the withheld details are relevant without being really mysterious to deserve the treatment.
+ The name of the Candidate
- It could be 'Prez Sark'

First of all comes to mind the actual name of the candidate. It says nothing to blank the name, and since it is a public figure and the containment includes that knowing the reality of the meme makes one more resistant to its effect, it comes weird to blank the name in a Foundation article. And since this guy have an outlandish platform, it clearly isn't the name of any particular individual of Real Life. It could even be an equally outlandish name.

+ Platform Document
- It could be fun to read

The documentation about the complete platform of this entity not only could be known, it could be present on the document, or appended as a separate article if it becomes too long. In the context of the anomaly it is far more relevant than the video, as much as the video is more interesting from our perspective, and even as an article it could be a way of showing several hints about this without really revealing nothing.

+ Final words in the screen
- Put something or get rid of them

At the end there is a redacted final words in the video. Why is that redacted? There is so many things that could be there, but given context, I don't really expect more than a name and a year. So, instead of creating mystery, it just says to me that they choose to redact a name that is actually public and easy in-character to find out.


Second, the classification and the containment procedures are really over the top, here, without necessity. Specially because it created a too large SCProcedure that ultimately gets in the way of the article instead of improving it. The anomaly isn't all that hard to find out and contain, neither is it so complex to do so. That's so serious that I almost downvoted it just because of this first part, while the main article is pretty good.
+ You could simplify this way
- And make it Euclid

Foundation agents and search engines are to keep surveillance of communications inside the USA at all times for the key words found on documentation about SCP-2078-1's platform, or its name. Personnel assigned to surveillance and containment of SCP-2078 should be of citizenship outside of the United States whenever possible. Agents embedded in the Federal Elections Commission (FEC) and American state governments are to intercept all attempts to register SCP-2078-1 as a candidate for election and reinforce surveillance during Election Day, with focus on polling places.

Infected subjects are to be administered Class-B amnestics and detained for observation for one week, then released if the infection subsides. Vectors and treatment-resistant subjects can be destroyed or contained through standard counter-memetic procedures in accord to local site's convenience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I feel like maybe 2078 is… something, some thing, that discovered America and fell in love with it as a concept without having any real comprehension of humanity. So 2078 is genuinely trying to be legitimately elected, on a platform based off of its own values and understanding of what happiness is, an understanding entirely orthogonal to any human values or understanding of happiness.

This only makes it all the more terrifying.

I am suddenly very glad that the year 2078 is not an election year.

item.title: Well Done.
item.content:encoded:
Something about the idea of millions of people just sitting and consuming, becoming nothing but mindless food and pleasure receptacles… I have this primal rejection to it. It just seems incredibly wrong. That's something few SCP's are able to do to me.

Nice job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That moment, when even Satan would be better president than Trump or Clinton.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No I mean, he uses language that would imply he doesn't like what he likes.

Also I don't know what you mean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2078-1: I firmly believe that America can make the right choice to reclaim our potential from the forces that have gripped this country for the past six thousand years.

What sort of entity do you think would be upset about how things have been going for the past six thousand years?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if I like how the entity is a card-carrying evil sort . E.g. "Their cries", "sour smelling place". I think it would be more effective if language like that was left out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's about time we started producing campaign materials for this.

IN EVERY POT
2016

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, still better than Trump.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Deez nuts, 2016" +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not an US citizen here, but it's so odd and freaky that I like it nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although RP fits the politics, this reminds me (and the letters behind the redaction match up to):

I'm pretty sure this is based on
Who first ran in 1992

…of Ross Perot. Who almost won. Except he dropped out of the race midway, only to come back after he'd lost all momentum. Seems to line up with having his supporters amnesticised while a media-blackout campaign was thrust upon him. He's gotten write-ins ever since.

Now I could be wrong…only Chubert knows for sure. Major +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like your suggestion.

I somehow neglected to upvote this when I originally read it. This problem has since been corrected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted when I realized what the skip is planning.

SPOILERZ
– hide block

You should work in the phrase "serving America" into it somehow, unless you think that makes the twist too obvious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… I don't understand this.
An MTF has a membership of I dunno, at the very most, a few hundred operatives. In what sense is it a problem to recruit those in a non-US english-speaking country, and have them work on containing the threat in the USA , without ever having citizenship?

I was OK with it since I assumed the US citizen members are there for some reasons regarding the effects of the SCP, perhaps identifying vectors, but this explanation makes no sense at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ideally, it'd be 0. Practically speaking, though, there's so many Americans that the MTF can't afford, logistically, to exclude them all by default.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A brilliant article with a very Twilight Zone feel to it.
I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
at no time are more than 49% of the membership of MTF Alpha-3 to possess American citizenship.

Shouldn't it 0% be American? Just to be safe?

and a constitutional amendment prohibiting vegetarianism

Do vegetarian and vegan infectees start eating meat?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry for the thread necro, but…damn now I am always going to read the addendum in RP's voice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is perfectly disturbing. Take my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have your +1, you magnificent bastard. I love SCPs that make me go "Ok…ok…ok…WHATTHEWHAT?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this would be another thing that only exists as a meme, but that bit at the end really tied it together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the end speech, but I feel like it kind of tips its hand a bit? Or maybe that it doesn't match the beginning tone? I'm not sure, so I'm not voting, but it doesn't quite work for me. Good format, tone, etc. though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So this is basically GOOGLE RON PAUL, the SCP right? Amazing and hilarious. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Intriguing idea. Good execution, too. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since this meme only affects people who self-identify as citizens of the USA, wouldn't it be useful for MTF Alpha-3 to involve a certain number of non-Americans?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of [insert political persons or party]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of course not. You lack vision. +1

Happy 100th!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a nice SCP. Very unsettling, especially with that last addendum. It strikes a very 1981 vibe, and I'd love to see someone write up something that suggests a connection between them. Come on, they're both SCPs that involve presidency and fucked-up speeches, clearly there's some connection here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Disturbing. +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please include some more significant content in a thread comment in the future. I…vaguely get what you're trying to say, but you should make a little more effort to communicate your feelings if they're significant enough to make a post in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mercia

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy as all get out.
Love it! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… Screw it. I'm voting this guy for President.

+1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The speech at the end could have been a lot more subtle, but I still really enjoyed this. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still a better candidate than Lyndon LaRouche…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the 1981 overtones I am getting here. Very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kill it.

With fire.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god, that final full paragraph of the transcript. Mouth was open by the end, that is great.

item.title: minor changes made
item.content:encoded:
1. I specified that the meme only affects people who self-identify as American, and

2. I nerfed the meme. I made it such that you have to be exposed to infection vectors for a certain amount of your day in order to infection to take. Hopefully, thi smakes containment a lot more plausible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Contrarily, I think getting this thing elected is a terrible idea (outside of maybe a Tale) because I'm having a hard enough time suspending disbelief for its concealment already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm with Flying Spidersnake. I -really- want to know what happens if he gets elected. The SCP itself felt somewhat weak, but I feel like that would make it stronger - tell us what would happen if this SCP was elected to a lower office, like mayor of a small town, or even to governor if you feel your authorial skills are up to the challenge.

That speech at the end makes me want more, and I think this is a case where giving a little more would actually improve the SCP considerably, as long as too much isn't revealed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [Delete this post please, just forgot for a second how tagging works]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, yeah, it reminds of a much much darker version of "Lose Now, Pay Later" by Carol Farley.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, it pleasantly surprised me. With the increased appetite and the birth requirement, I was half expecting to have to down-vote baby-eating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that was a farmer speaking…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So much this. I spent the entire article going "Oh, so it's contagious agrarian politics. Eh."

But then that ending. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just glad it didn't turn into one big Ron Paul joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the most of the article isn't quite as strong as it could be, but the last chunk of the speech got me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So he wants people to become battery hens? Now there's a policy I can support.

But this article really makes me feel it would have been pretty awesome if the author had taken it in any direction, though I do love the ending.

It would be interestingly horrific if the person causing the effect was just a guy who wants to make things better. Imagine the horror of being a decent person, eminently qualified to hold office, who has to be locked up forever because someone merely knowing your name convinces them to vote for you.

But it would work just as well if the candidate was some neighbourhood crank. Someone who doesn't want to be a politician at all, but whose neighbours keep nominating for president. Or maybe someone about whom the Foundation doesn't know anything except that he has this effect, because he and his beliefs proved so contagious they just locked him up in a room with no doors and dosed everyone, themselves included, with amnestics to forget everything about him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well that was…disturbing. Even more so because I'm getting the subtle feeling that there's a darker purpose to the fattening up of the American populace. Well written and enjoyable.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, contrary to others, I found the speech got a bit too OH MY GOD LOOK AT THE HORROR as it went on. I think it would have been better with a bit more subtlety.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: awesome as fawk. if he gets elected, don't blame me, I voted for [REDACTED]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy hell I'm glad I live in Europe. Furthermore, don't you EVER do a version of this guy applied to Mardi Gras. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still better than [politician of your choice here]. Also, i'm a downright sucker for speeches like the ending. I really want to see what happens if he/it gets elected. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how I feel about this article. I like the idea, but I have a personal bias against scips that could very reasonably have horrible unstoppable consequences. But, I'm fond of the idea of the president being an alien/demon/thing that seizes power and mucks up the country. No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: calls to mind Ronald Reagan All Cut Up in a very good way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love me some good old-fashioned creep.
